intro.description: This cheat sheet guides you through using the JFace Cleanup to modernize JFace API usage in Eclipse plugin code, replacing deprecated APIs with their modern equivalents.

item.title: Understand JFace Modernization
item.description:
The JFace API has evolved over multiple Eclipse versions. Several APIs are deprecated and should be replaced:
• ViewerSorter → ViewerComparator (deprecated since Eclipse 3.3)
• IProgressMonitor usage patterns modernized with null-safe helpers
• Image descriptors updated for HiDPI support
• Old dialog and wizard patterns simplified
Using the modern APIs improves compatibility with current Eclipse versions and avoids deprecation warnings.

item.title: Enable the Cleanup
item.description:
Open the Clean Up dialog to enable the JFace Cleanup:
1. In the Package Explorer, select an Eclipse plugin project using JFace
2. Choose Source → Clean Up…
3. Select Use custom profile and click Configure…
4. On the JFace Cleanup (Sandbox) tab, enable the desired modernization options
5. Click OK
Click Perform to open the Source menu.

item.title: Run the Cleanup on Eclipse Plugin Code
item.description:
Apply the JFace cleanup to your plugin source files:
1. Open or select Java files in your Eclipse plugin that use JFace APIs
2. Choose Source → Clean Up…
3. Confirm the profile has JFace Cleanup (Sandbox) enabled
4. Click Finish
The cleanup replaces deprecated JFace APIs with their modern equivalents automatically.

item.title: Review the Changes
item.description:
After the cleanup runs, inspect the updated code:
• ViewerSorter subclasses are migrated to extend ViewerComparator
• Deprecated image loading calls are updated for HiDPI / Retina display support
• Progress monitor patterns are modernized
Build the plugin and run it to verify the changes work correctly in the Eclipse runtime. Use Run → Run As → Eclipse Application to launch a test instance.
